channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Tarkov Arena’s Biggest Patch Yet Has Just Arrived
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has deployed the 0.5.0 update across Escape from Tarkov: Arena. This session-based, hardcore shooter is the traditional multiplayer portion of the extraction shooter’s universe, offering fast-paced, small-form action over the expansive, open adventures found in Escape from Tarkov, the core platform.

The 0.5.0.0 update in EFT: Arena represents a massive overhaul to the game’s ecosystem. It adds an entirely new battle pass, new maps, refined modes, fresh weapons, hundreds of rewards, and much more.

EFT: Arena’s 0.5.0.0 Patch is Enormous

Escape from Tarkov: Arena doesn’t have the pull that the core platform does, but it’s still a nice supplementary title, especially for those already loving Escape from Tarkov. The team at Battlestate Games has linked the two games together in a few ways, which means what you do in Arena can benefit your ‘main character’ in Escape from Tarkov.

Recently, the team rolled out the Casus Belli event, designed to cross the wires between the two games, and it was a pretty big success.

In the most recent Escape from Tarkov: Arena patch notes for the 0.5.0 update, we get a look at just how gargantuan this change is.

It starts with the new battle pass, also named Casus Belli. This features more than 70 unique rewards, including weapon skins, new sights and attachments, fresh clothing, and full gear sets for players to unlock and use as they grind the levels.

Factory has been introduced to Escape from Tarkov: Arena as a new tactical map. It has a unique voiceover, courtesy of Tagilla, but to access the map, which includes new points of interest and exclusive rewards, you’ll need to buy it on the Escape from Tarkov website.

ShootOut has been reworked and brought back for this new season in EFT: Arena. This is a mini-tournament mode that offers fast-paced action among six teams of two players each. It has been fully redesigned and improved for the 0.5.0 patch.

Additionally, EFT: Arena gets several audio changes, including cutting down gunshot audio and the range of certain impact sounds.

To further flesh out the platform, Battlestate has introduced a massive 46-part task line in EFT: Arena. The first tier of this is designed to help beginners understand the game, while the Advanced and Hardcore levels provide missions for the more talented players in the Arena.

Here are all the new weapons added to EFT: Arena with this patch:

  • Colt M16A1
  • Colt M16A2 (in the Mk12 Mod 0 marksman rifle preset variant)
  • CGNL NL545 (DI)
  • CGNL NL545 (GP)
  • AK-308
  • AS VAL MOD.4
  • Marlin MXLR
  • KAC SR-25 (Taupe) (in M110 preset variants)
  • TheAKGuy AK-50
  • TKPD carbine

This list is in addition to dozens of armor items, attachments, and other pieces of equipment, all brought over from Escape from Tarkov to bolster the offering in EFT: Arena.

But that’s still not everything.

Meta score has been rebalanced, starter presets have been adjusted, weapon and attachments progression has been overhauled, unpopular locations in certain modes have been disabled from the map pool, deeper customization options have been added, and, of course, the game has now transitioned to Unity 6.

That means the team has:

  • Improved performance and overall client stability.
  • Fixed several issues from the previous Unity version.
  • Unlocked potential for further performance optimization and graphical improvements.

Before we wrap, here’s what we mean by the Meta score element:

With Patch 0.5.0, we are introducing experimental changes to the Meta score limits. Previously, the limits of each of the four bars had to be considered when assembling a valid preset, helping maintain balance and variety in EFT: Arena. At the same time, the system felt overloaded, especially for new players.

We are temporarily removing restrictions for three Meta score bars: Firepower, Defense, and Utility. This will give players complete freedom in building presets, but may result in unbalanced “meme” builds appearing in matches.

Are you still playing EFT: Arena? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re excited about this massive change.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about Escape from Tarkov’s latest meta shift

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/tarkov-arena-biggest-patch-yet-arrived/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Complete Saros Trophy List—How To Get All Trophies
item.content:encoded:
The Saros trophy list is in keeping with typical Housemarque trophy lists, offering a mix of challenging tasks, but difficult enough to make you feel accomplished if you can mop them up all and claim the Platinum Trophy.

Saros offers even more punishing, pixel-perfect roguelite perfection with Housemarque at the wheel. Living up to the sky-high expectations of Returnal isn’t easy, but I’m more than confident that Saros will be another world-beating, system-selling behemoth. If you plan on buying Saros, you should check out the trophy list below.

*THERE WILL BE STORY SPOILERS, SO BE WARNED*

How Many Achievements & Trophies In Saros?

The Saros trophy list is broken up into 45 Trophies, with the list divided into 1 Platinum Trophy, 2 Gold, 16 Silver, and 26 Bronze.

Insider Gaming has been sent the Saros trophy list ahead of time by an anonymous source, which is why I’m able to bring this to you now. For comparison, Saros’ trophy list is a seven-trophy upgrade on Returnal (only offering 38 Trophies), giving you even more incredible accomplishments to hunt for.

  • More Insider Gaming: Saros Review Embargo Date Countdown

Better yet, from the outside, there seems to be less focus on random trophies like the eternal headaches Returnal’s Past the Ruins trophy caused. Obviously, there are only trophies to earn, as Saros’ platforms are very limited.

Once again, this is your final warning before I reveal the full Saros trophy list to you, and it will contain all 45 Trophies, including story trophies and more.

All Saros Trophies & Achievements

Here’s every Saros trophy to obtain.

  • The Sun Is Forever: Mastered Carcaso and obtained every SAROS trophy.
  • Prophet: Defeat the Overlord of the Shattered Rise.
  • Bastion: Defeat the Overlord of the Ancient Depths.
  • Rhabdom: Defeat the Overlord of the Shattered Descent.
  • Legion: Defeat the Overlord of the Blighted Marsh.
  • Architect: Defeat the Overlord of the Desecrated Fortress.
  • Shepherd: Defeat the Overlord of the Acolyte’s Haven.
  • Priestess: Defeat the Overlord of the Cathedral.
  • King: Defeat the Overlord of the Yellow Shore.
  • Let Go: Complete all Epilogue requirements and defeat the King.
  • Never Let Go: Complete Act 1.
  • Into Yellow: Complete Act 2.
  • The Blazing Throne: Complete Act 3.
  • Strange Is The Night: Enter the Banyan Tree for the first time.
  • Shattered Glass: Complete every encounter inside the Banyan Tree.
  • Nightmare Strands: Enter and complete all 7 Nightmare Gates at least once.
  • Full Arsenal: Unlock all Main and Power Weapons, and defeat 25 hostiles with each.
  • Bullet Paradise: Dispatch 1995 regular hostiles.
  • Carcosan Cartographer: Use the Carcosan Modifiers to embark upon and survive 33 expeditions.
  • Soltari R&D: Unlock and utilize all Traits and Variants of a Weapon.
  • Break The Cycle: Absorb 2021 projectiles using the Shield.
  • Reinforced Carapace: Collect 20 Integrity Augments.
  • Eclipse’s Gift: Collect 70 Halcyon.
  • Carcosan Conqueror: Encounter and defeat 10 Alpha hostiles.
  • Catch And Release: Use the Shield to absorb 3 Power bars, then defeat a hostile with a Power
    Weapon.
  • Untouchable: Complete a Biome without taking direct, non-Corruption damage or losing
    Adrenaline.
  • Second Chance: Use Second Chance for the first time.
  • Approach Vector: Dispatch a shielded hostile with a mid-air Melee attack.
  • OverDriven: Dispatch 30 hostiles using OverDrive using a single cycle.
  • Countermand: Stagger 2 hostiles simultaneously with one Parry.
  • Enforcer: Damage 7 hostiles within 5 seconds with Handcannons.
  • Relentless: Land 50 consecutive shots on hostiles with Rifles.
  • Spread the Cost: Land 7 consecutive Shotgun blasts with 100% pellet accuracy.
  • Vicious Circle: Use Chakrams to damage 4 hostiles within 2 seconds.
  • All Shall Bow: Hit 3 hostiles at once with Crossbows.
  • Broadside: Defeat 5 hostiles with a single Prominence blast.
  • Atrophy: Damage 5 hostiles simultaneously with Dispiritor projectiles.
  • Angle Of Attack: Defeat a hostile using a ricochet Nova Lance slug.
  • Radiance: Damage 3 hostiles simultaneously using the Illumine.
  • Fatal Triptych: Die 3 times during a single cycle.
  • War Within: Reach maximum Corruption level and survive.
  • Never Enough: Collect 20 Halcyon in the Yellow Shore.
  • Fall From Grace: Use the environment to eliminate a hostile without shooting it.
  • Vincible: Complete a Biome without using the Shield.
  • Come Back Stronger: Die for the first time.

What do you make of the Saros trophy list? How does it compare to Returnal’s? Do you think you’ll be getting the Platinum Trophy? Share your thoughts with the team and me via the Insider Gaming Discord, where we do respond to messages!

Check out more Returnal guides:

  • Is Saros A Sequel To Returnal?
  • All Saros PS5 Pro Enhanced Features
  • Saros Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
  • Does Saros Have Early Access or a Demo?
  • Saros Game File Size and Pre-Load Date

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Escape from Tarkov’s Latest Patch Notes Reveal Another Meta Change
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has ushered in another effort to eradicate the Escape from Tarkov meta. This time, the team is challenging the dominance of suppressed weapons, targeting the impact such attachments have on the overall build of guns in the popular extraction shooter.

The latest Escape from Tarkov patch notes, which make up the 1.0.4.5 update, reveal deeper optimizations, including those that impact the out-of-raid experience, and more balancing across stacks of weapons.

Escape from Tarkov’s 1.0.4.5 Patch Brings Fresh Improvements

Escape from Tarkov has undergone a major change today, with Battlestate Games taking the game offline for several hours to apply the 1.0.4.5 patch. It’s the middle of the road until we hit 1.0.5, which will introduce the Icebreaker map and a new boss, amongst other things.

Today’s update is pretty huge, and it shifts the meta again.

With this change, players will find that barrels, handguards, and muzzle devices have all been reworked. Hanguards will no longer reduce recoil, but will now reduce ergo more. Similarly, suppressors and compensators will have less of an impact on recoil, but will lean more towards the ergonomic measurements of your custom builds.

The team explained:

These changes are intended to further increase the popularity of unsuppressed weapon builds. Adjustments to muzzle flash are planned for future updates.

Further, a huge array of weapons had their base recoil and ergonomics stats addressed:

  • All AK, AKM, and AK-100 series weapons
  • Steyr AUG A1
  • Steyr AUG A3
  • HK G36
  • HK 416A5
  • FN SCAR-L
  • FN SCAR-H
  • SIG MCX-SPEAR
  • SIG MCX .300
  • CMMG Mk47 Mutant
  • Rifle Dynamics RD-704
  • Aklys Defense Velociraptor
  • All machine guns
  • All bolt-action and marksman rifles chambered in 7.62×51, 7.62x54R, .338, and .50 BMG

In terms of wider optimizations, PvE players now have disconnect protection on their client-side matches. This ensures that, if they disconnect while in a raid, their task progress will revert to the state it was at the start of the raid, avoiding failures.

The player culling system has now been applied to more maps: Lighthouse, Shoreline, Woods, and Ground Zero. The full suite of maps will get this treatment in future iterations of Escape from Tarkov, the team promised.

Improvements have also been deployed to out-of-raid screens, with the FPS limit in menus having been increased and both the animation and audio engines being improved to reduce PC resource usage.

Here’s the full sweep of bug fixes introduced with 1.0.4.5:

  • Fixed an issue where dead players and bots could appear to move visually in certain cases
  • Fixed an issue where damage would not register after a weapon malfunction if the player had previously used a stationary weapon
  • Fixed the cause of error 228 during trader dialogues that could block task progression
  • Fixes to culling, shadows, textures, and location geometry
  • Fixed an issue where armor could not be picked up if it was dropped while interacting with the modification window
  • Fixed the incorrect behavior of PMC bots toward player Scavs with Fence reputation 6 or higher in the PvE Zone
  • Fixed an issue preventing further progression in The Labyrinth after spawning in the room requiring a burner tool to exit
  • Fixed the incorrect display of smoke grenades in the player’s hands if another player threw a smoke grenade at them
  • Fixed the display of changes in the “Combat Skill Leveling Boost” field in the Hall of Fame Hideout zone when one dogtag was replaced with another of a similar level
  • Fixed an issue where PostFX settings would not display when preview was enabled
  • Fixed an issue where some players could get stuck in the secret saferoom on Interchange without a way to exit
  • Fixed the incorrect display of stationary weapons used by bots and multiple cases where bots could float while using them
  • Fixed multiple issues with the incorrect behavior of loot and gear items when dropped in a raid
  • Fixed the display of the current movement noise level and stance height during raids
  • Fixed the third-person animation for inspecting your own inventory.

This update is the preface to 1.0.5, which will be the largest Escape from Tarkov update since it was released fully in November 2025. Let us know what you’re most looking forward to about 1.0.5 on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Metro’s creator doesn’t think 2039 is ‘just a game’

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Does Forza Horizon 6 Have Early Access?
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 is screeching into view and stands as one of the biggest games set to be released in 2026, and, as is customary with modern releases, questions are floating around regarding the early access availability.

Early access has become a popular approach in modern gaming, with many developers charging an extra fee to be among the first wave of players in the game, and Xbox is utilising this approach with increasing frequency.

If you’re looking for all the details on Forza Horizon 6’s early access availability, Insider Gaming has everything you need to know, including the full costs of the privilege.

Does Forza Horizon 6 Have Early Access?

Yes, Forza Horizon 6 has early access available, with eligible players able to play the full game up to four days earlier than the official May 19 release date.

This shouldn’t come as much of a surprise to long-term fans of the franchise, as other Forza Horizon games, alongside Forza Motorsport games, have offered similar early availability.

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

However, as is often the case with modern gaming, additional days of access for Forza Horizon 6 come at a cost, requiring players to fork out a significant outlay for the bonus.

How to Get Forza Horizon 6 Early Access

Forza Horizon 6 early access is only available through purchasing the Premium Edition of the title, which offers the four-day period among its main benefits.

Gamers accessing Forza Horizon 6 through an Xbox Game Pass Ultimate or PC Game Pass subscription will not be able to play in early access either, unless they directly upgrade to the Premium Edition.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Xbox Game Pass?

The Premium Edition of Forza Horizon 6 comes with a significant price tag of $119.99, but there is a cheaper version available for those who already have access to the Standard Edition through Game Pass.

Those with access through Game Pass can purchase the Premium Edition Upgrade, which costs $59.99, considerably less than the full price of the Premium Edition.

Which version of Forza Horizon 6 are you planning to pick up? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, take a look at the platform availability for the title, and check out the full list of achievements.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/forza-horizon-6-early-access/

item.title: Shuhei Yoshida Was Fired From PlayStation Because He ‘Didn’t Listen’ To Jim Ryan
item.content:encoded:
Shuhei Yoshida was the heart and soul of Sony’s PlayStation division, serving as head of Sony Worldwide Studios, but we’ve learned that the industry icon was fired for not following the commands of Jim Ryan and his need for Yoshida to do some ‘ridiculous’ things.

Some of PlayStation’s biggest exclusives, AA, and indie successes have been overseen by Shuhei Yoshida. He’s been everywhere for Sony and PlayStation, from wanting Stellar Blade on PlayStation after playing it to leading PlayStation’s indie game development.

We’ve learned more about why Yoshida’s role in the company changed drastically, though.

Jim Ryan Fired Shuhei Yoshida For Not Doing ‘Ridiculous’ Things

In a story from This Week In Videogames, we learned that Shuhei Yoshida was recently speaking at the Australian games festival ALT: GAMES.

Yoshida opened up on his role as head of Sony Worldwide Studios (which you may now know as PlayStation Studios), and how, CEO at the time, Jim Ryan and he weren’t seeing eye to eye on things:

“I helped Santa Monica to make God of War, Naughty Dog to make Uncharted and The Last of Us, and Sucker Punch to make the beautiful Ghost of Tsushima. Ghost of Tsushima was one of the last games that I worked on as the president of Worldwide Studios. But in 2019, after 11 years leading the first-party development, I was fired from the role.

Jim Ryan wanted to remove me from first-party because I didn’t listen to him. He asked to do some ridiculous things, and I said ‘No.”

It’s not clear what those ‘ridiculous’ things were, but we do know that it ultimately resulted in Yoshida being moved to PlayStation’s Indie division. He would then leave PlayStation in January 2025.

Jim Ryan has also left the company, stepping down as CEO.

What are your favorite Shuhei Yoshida moments from down the years? Is there one game that stands out above the rest that he helped bring to life? Did Sony mistreat him? Let us know your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about Pragmata selling over one million copies in two days and Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 coming in 2027.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Xbox Game Pass?
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 is racing toward the starting block, bringing a brand-new adventure in Japan for players, and, as with every Xbox release, questions arise about the title’s Xbox Game Pass status.

Xbox Game Pass has proven to be a controversial topic among gamers, especially considering recent price hikes, but the premise of providing free access for subscribers to a host of games makes it a must-have entity for many.

If you’re wondering whether Forza Horizon 6 is available on Game Pass, and what tier you need to have, Insider Gaming has all the details.

Can You Play Forza Horizon 6 on Game Pass?

Yes, Forza Horizon 6 is available to play on Xbox Game Pass without a separate purchase, though there are some limitations.

Xbox Game Pass’s various price tiers mean not every subscription will be eligible to play Forza Horizon 6 for free, and some may have to pay the up-front cost to gain access to the game.

The good news is, progress is saved across all devices via the Xbox Play Anywhere service, so you won’t have to fork out twice if you want to play on more than one platform.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Forza Horizon 6 on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained

What Version of Forza Horizon 6 is on Game Pass?

Game Pass membership includes only the Standard Edition of Forza Horizon 6, and, therefore, does not provide four days of early access.

The Premium Edition of Forza Horizon 6 opens the door for players on May 15, four days ahead of the full release, but these benefits are not available for those with a Game Pass subscription.

However, rather than forking out for the Premium Edition in full, those with Standard Edition access through Xbox Game Pass can purchase the Premium Edition Upgrade Bundle for $59.99.

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

What Game Pass Subscription Tier is Needed For Forza Horizon 6?

Forza Horizon 6 is only available to Xbox Game Pass subscribers with either an Ultimate or PC Game Pass subscription.

Microsoft’s changes to the Game Pass structure in October 2025 mean only Ultimate Game Pass subscribers have access to Forza Horizon 6, and other first-party titles, on their launch day.

Those with a Premium Game Pass subscription will gain access to Forza Horizon 6 within a year of launch, but those with an Essential Game Pass subscription won’t have access to Forza Horizon 6.

Are you splashing out on the Premium Edition of Forza Horizon 6? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, take a look at the full list of achievements, and check out the limited edition Xbox accessories.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/forza-horizon-6-game-pass/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Forza Horizon 6 on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 is screeching around the final corner and heading into the final stretch before release, and the platform availability variations mean preparation for launch is different for many players.

The Playground Games title is the latest entry in a franchise that has dominated the racing scene, growing to be even more popular than the Forza Motorsport series, which it was a spin-off from.

If you’re ready to get behind the wheel in Forza Horizon 6 but want to know all the platform details, Insider Gaming has all you need to know.

Is Forza Horizon 6 on PlayStation?

No, Forza Horizon 6 is not playable on PlayStation when it releases on May 19, but it is confirmed to be coming to the platform later down the line.

While PlayStation 4 players will have to go without, the door was opened to a PlayStation 5 release when Forza Horizon 5 launched on the Sony platform in April 2025. That set a new precedent for the franchise, which Forza Horizon 6 will follow.

Although no precise launch date has been shared for Forza Horizon 6 on PlayStation 5, it was confirmed in January that a launch will occur before the end of the calendar year.

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Is Forza Horizon 6 on Xbox?

Yes, Forza Horizon 6 is available to play on Xbox Series X|S, but the title is a current-gen exclusive, meaning Xbox One players will miss out.

This marks the first time that the latest title in the franchise has not released on Xbox One, with Forza Horizon 5 having been playable on last-gen consoles, but it shouldn’t come as much of a surprise, given how deep Microsoft is into the X|S lifespan.

However, the Xbox Cloud Gaming service means Xbox One players should be able to stream Forza Horizon 6 directly over the internet, bypassing downloads and storage limitations, to enjoy the game in full.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete Forza Horizon 6 Achievement List

Is Forza Horizon 6 on PC?

Yes, Forza Horizon 6 will be available to play on PC when it officially launches, with the drop occurring simultaneously with the Xbox Series X|S release.

PC gamers have full access to the Xbox library, including the benefit of having Forza Horizon 6 available with a Game Pass subscription, and the Cloud Gaming service means you can stream the game if your PC is not powerful enough to run the game natively.

The release marks the next step in the Forza Horizon franchise’s release on PC, which has launched the same day as Xbox platforms in every edition since Forza Horizon 3.

Which platform are you planning to play Forza Horizon 6 on? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, take a look at the limited edition Xbox accessories, and have a glance at the full reveal of the Japan map.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
